cards.title: Adrenochrome
cards.omen: The swine are running. Follow them.
cards.lore: Somewhere past the third drink he stopped being a man and became pure bad craziness with fists. Don't make eye contact — he can smell fear, and he is very, very thirsty.

cards.title: Ether
cards.omen: Wake me when the acid wears off.
cards.lore: He took the whole bag before noon and now the odds bend around his yawn like heat off the highway. Wake him and you will answer for it.

cards.title: Barstow
cards.omen: Buy the ticket, brand the sky.
cards.lore: We were somewhere around the county line when the pepper started talking, and it has not shut up since. He herds sparks the way lesser men herd regrets.

cards.title: Doom
cards.omen: Everything worth having is buried.
cards.lore: Two hundred pounds of grievance and a pickaxe he calls the American Dream. He digs down because down is where they buried the good stuff.

cards.title: Simmer
cards.omen: Patience, in a tall white hat.
cards.lore: He has been simmering the same pot since the Fear set in and swears it is almost ready. Nobody is allowed to look under the lid. Nobody wants to.

cards.title: Freaks
cards.omen: Two wrongs and a rental car.
cards.lore: One runs hot, one runs foul, and neither remembers whose idea this was. Together they are worth the whole degenerate weekend.

cards.title: Meltdown
cards.omen: I'm still cooling. Don't touch.
cards.lore: They pressed him too hot and he never set right; he drips a little more each block, grinning through the runoff. Hold him too long and you will be scraping him off the felt.

cards.title: Baron
cards.omen: The house of the dead is hiring.
cards.lore: He files every card that dies in battle under the brim of his hat, and business is booming. Every burn is a new tenant in the house of the doomed.

cards.title: Dignity
cards.omen: At least die with clean pants.
cards.lore: Half a funeral and half a punchline, hitching up his pants for a duel he lost before the ether wore off. He fights anyway. That's the whole gag.

cards.title: Grin
cards.omen: Everything ends grinning.
cards.lore: Nothing left but a smile and a pair of gloves, dissolving pleasant as ether. He is the last thing a burned card sees before the lights go savage.

cards.title: Vig
cards.omen: The contract's in the teeth.
cards.lore: He loans favors and collects in centuries, smiling the whole brutal way down. Sign nothing you can't out-run.

cards.title: Stew
cards.omen: Trust the chef. Fear the meat.
cards.lore: He knows exactly what's in the pot and finds your paranoia delicious. Ask no questions, eat fast, tip well.

cards.title: Feast
cards.omen: Nobody leaves the table sober.
cards.lore: He can't stop plating, because a stopped feast is a dead weekend and this one is not dying on his watch. His grin is a promise there's always another course of bad decisions.

cards.title: Breakfast
cards.omen: The sun is a slower oven.
cards.lore: He worked the graveyard shift straight into morning and never noticed the sun come up screaming. Breakfast, to him, is just last night that survived.

cards.title: Quickdraw
cards.omen: Draw. I already did.
cards.lore: Quicker to the pistol than the punchline, he settles every tie with a coin only he can see land. The chrome remembers every duel; the kid remembers none.

cards.title: Odds
cards.omen: I know. I always know.
cards.lore: He can smell a bluff across a smoke-filled room and he will never tell you how. His silence is the most expensive thing in the deck.

cards.title: Static
cards.omen: Good boy. Now feed the fire.
cards.lore: Stitched from candy and bad reception, he begs for burns instead of biscuits. Feed him and he glows like a motel sign at 3 AM.

cards.title: Upward
cards.omen: Losing is just slow winning.
cards.lore: Too dumb to lose and too lucky to notice, he stumbles through every season and lands in the winning pack. God protects fools, and this one specifically.

cards.title: Monster
cards.omen: The biggest ones only want holding.
cards.lore: Proof that even the big ugly ones just want holding at the end of a long weekend. Whoever holds him gathers all the gentle freaks.

cards.title: Rainbow
cards.omen: Open wide. The light tastes fine.
cards.lore: All grin and no face, chewing the color right out of the dark. He eats the dull cards and belches something you'll see for days.

cards.title: Bullion
cards.omen: I was here before the Fear.
cards.lore: Frozen mid-thought since before the first bad idea, he's the deck's steadiest floor. Gold doesn't burn; it just changes hands in the dark.

cards.title: Wound
cards.omen: Some wounds are load-bearing.
cards.lore: He guards the knife-stuck heart that started this whole degenerate game and won't let it scab over. As long as it bleeds, the weekend's still alive.

cards.title: Noon
cards.omen: I'm the joke the desert tells.
cards.lore: Day laughs, night grins, and the poor bastard in between juggles a light nobody else can hold. He is the punchline the sky's been building to.

cards.title: Shades
cards.omen: We're the part you can't quite see.
cards.lore: Nobody's seen their faces and nobody's survived asking. Worth double, but only if you're fool enough to wager both.

cards.title: Shovel
cards.omen: Nothing's buried. It's just resting.
cards.lore: He digs up the cards the deck tried to bury, and the deck keeps burying more. One down, two up, all night, every night.

cards.title: Fear
cards.omen: Blink first. Everyone does.
cards.lore: He works the losing side of the ledger, feeding on everything forfeited to the dark. Meet his eye and the bet is already placed.

cards.title: Loyalty
cards.omen: Wager one, wager both.
cards.lore: Two mutts split down the middle and welded back by pure devotion. Try to trade one and they'll both sulk you into the ground.

cards.title: Unrendered
cards.omen: We're the sketch before the ink.
cards.lore: Two spirits still waiting on their color, sketched at the edge of a bad trip. Hold them and you decide what crawls out.

cards.title: Chorus
cards.omen: When we croak, it turns.
cards.lore: They only turn up in threes and only sing when the gas runs high and the Fear runs higher. A full chorus means the weekend's about to turn savage.

cards.title: Totem
cards.omen: The top is borrowed from the bottom.
cards.lore: A pillar of degenerates, each holding up the one above and denying they're touching. Pull the bottom and the whole tower answers at once.

cards.title: Scorch
cards.omen: Catch me and you'll get the smoke.
cards.lore: Too quick to catch and far too cool to care, he outruns his own flames down the highway. Chase him and you'll collect nothing but scorch marks and regret.

cards.title: Bender
cards.omen: The dice were never yours.
cards.lore: He casts the odds themselves, bending a doomed hand into a winning one for the price of a burn. The dice were never yours to roll, friend.

cards.title: Phoenix
cards.omen: I'm the last page and the first.
cards.lore: Every season he burns to the felt and claws back out of the ashes of the last pack. Own him and you own an ending, plus the ugly morning after.

cards.title: Joy
cards.omen: Scream if you're glad you're a card.
cards.lore: Delight so violent it registers as a small explosion in the parking lot. His good mood raises the temperature of every card near enough to catch it.

cards.title: Comedown
cards.omen: Five more blocks. Please.
cards.lore: He hoards the dead hours between weekends under his pillow like contraband. Wake him and the next season starts early and mean.

cards.title: Vermin
cards.omen: The moon owes me. It knows.
cards.lore: He gnaws the frayed edges of the pack where the rare ones come loose. Whatever that filthy moon shows him, he lifts before dawn.

cards.title: Prophet
cards.omen: The way out is the way it points.
cards.lore: His limbs bend in directions nobody approved, and so do his predictions. Follow the finger, never the face.

cards.title: Guffaw
cards.omen: HA — and the pack turns over.
cards.lore: One howl so enormous it warps the wallpaper and the odds with it. When he laughs, the whole deck feels the shove and reshuffles.

cards.title: Gulper
cards.omen: It all fits if you don't chew.
cards.lore: He swallows the little cards whole and grins with the evidence still showing. A sloppy eater with a suspiciously solid floor.

cards.title: Hare
cards.omen: Eh. What's the wager?
cards.lore: Cool under any wager and sitting on the punchline like a loaded gun. Bet against him and you become the joke, doc.

cards.title: Crimson
cards.omen: I found it! I found all of it!
cards.lore: A tower of red fur and pure uncut enthusiasm, fetching the cards you didn't know you'd lost. He means well. That's the terrifying part.

cards.title: Barnyard
cards.omen: I say — the pack is OPEN.
cards.lore: Loudest mouth in the yard, first to a duel, first to crow the weekend open. All swagger, all bandana, no brakes.

cards.title: Fingers
cards.omen: You won't miss it. Trust me.
cards.lore: One of a gang that lifts cards clean off the unwary between drinks. Watch your pack when he's in the pull.

cards.title: Highway
cards.omen: Twenty-one steps, then start again.
cards.lore: He walks the long numbered road between weekends, muttering a count he half-remembers. Each step is a prayer and a bad decision.

cards.title: King
cards.omen: Long may I — eh, whatever.
cards.lore: Crowned too young, grinning too wide, ruling the whole weekend from a throne of black-light and static. His court is small and loud and doomed.

cards.title: Brute
cards.omen: Take it. It only works if given.
cards.lore: Big, blue, and dangerously honest, wearing his heart right where you can wager it. Win it and the fool thing beats for you.

cards.title: Crossing
cards.omen: Cross me and find out.
cards.lore: He walks the black gap between two weekends and dares you to follow into bat country. Luck turns the instant he passes — you just won't know which way.

cards.title: Cuddly
cards.omen: Pet me. Go on. Pet me.
cards.lore: Adorable right up until the foil dims, then it's all teeth and bad craziness. Feed after the burn window at your own funeral.

cards.title: Swine
cards.omen: We're of three minds about this.
cards.lore: Past, present, and pack, seated as one and disagreeing softly through the ether. He votes three times and burns exactly once.

cards.title: Spook
cards.omen: Boo. Also, nice hand.
cards.lore: Death came for him and he laughed and kept the shades. Now he haunts the winners' side, grinning at the loot.

cards.title: Counsel
cards.omen: The fine print is where I live.
cards.lore: He reads the rulebook so no one else has to, then quietly rewrites a clause in his favor. Refined, patient, and always one edition ahead of the law.

cards.title: Approval
cards.omen: Looks good to me. It's always on fire.
cards.lore: Approval, fully engulfed. His thumbs-up is worth a small fortune and a large hospital bill.

cards.title: Trouble
cards.omen: One puff, one card, no refunds.
cards.lore: He blows rings that come back as new cards and trouble that comes back as more trouble. Deal with him and count your pack twice, then again.

cards.title: Spun
cards.omen: Round and round and rare.
cards.lore: Whirled around one time too many and stuck grinning with the room still moving. He makes the whole board a little woozier just by breathing.

cards.title: Drifter
cards.omen: No pack. No season. Just the road.
cards.lore: He wandered in from the ragged edge of the set with no season, no hurry, and no fixed address. Whatever pack he drifts into gets weird and stays weird.

cards.title: Eruption
cards.omen: Everything old is lava now.
cards.lore: A season ending and beginning in the same molten breath, straight up from the earth's bad conscience. Hold him at the lock and feel the ground go savage.

cards.title: Mirrors
cards.omen: The same light, twice.
cards.lore: Drawn in nothing but light and doubled on a checkered floor at the end of the trip. A prizm pair — proof the foil can be pure, weightless line.

cards.title: Coronation
cards.omen: Crowned in light, light as luck.
cards.lore: A crowning drawn entirely in glowing wire, crown and all, radiating out of the dark. The rarest way this degenerate deck says: this one rules.

cards.title: Glow
cards.omen: Bright together or not at all.
cards.lore: Line-art ghosts that only fully arrive when the foil runs hot and the hour runs late. Catch them lit and you've caught a prizm.

cards.title: Elders
cards.omen: Last light. Then the dark.
cards.lore: The oldest spirits redrawn as pure radiance, presiding over the frayed edge of the set. A prizm chase and the deck's quiet, doomed finale.

cards.title: Waltz
cards.omen: I'll be a puddle by the chorus.
cards.lore: He keeps dancing even as he drips, one glove up, tongue out, unbothered by his own dissolution. The runoff pools into whatever comes next.

cards.title: Filament
cards.omen: I'm the diagram of a laugh.
cards.lore: Unspooled into pure gold line, grinning from inside the wireframe of the whole rotten world. Pull one thread and a season unravels in your hands.

cards.title: Garden
cards.omen: The garden keeps its own book.
cards.lore: He tends the flowers that grow between weekends, where the rare cards bloom like bad ideas. Pick nothing without asking the garden first.

cards.title: Riddle
cards.omen: The answer's a better riddle.
cards.lore: Every answer he gives is a worse question wearing a nicer hat. Solve him and he just asks you something with teeth.

cards.title: Unfinished
cards.omen: Finish me, or forget me.
cards.lore: A card the printer never colored in, still waiting at the edge of a comedown. Hold him and you owe the deck a face.

cards.title: Sharp
cards.omen: Pick a card. I already know it.
cards.lore: He shuffles fate for a living and always deals himself the top of the pack. Cut the deck — he'll cut it right back.

cards.title: Scarlet
cards.omen: Magic is just patience, lit.
cards.lore: Patient, exacting, and one incantation ahead of every other bastard in the room. He lights one spark and calls it a plan.

cards.title: Crow
cards.omen: Rise and shine and sugar.
cards.lore: Loud as sin and drowning happily in a field of candy, crowing the pack awake. Every jellybean he stands on is a weekend he outlasted.

cards.title: Wrung
cards.omen: I hold more than I look like.
cards.lore: Squeezed dry and smiling anyway, he soaks up whatever the season pours on him. Wring him out and last week's luck runs down the drain.

cards.title: Spark
cards.omen: Wait — is that ME burning?
cards.lore: Forever surprised to find himself on fire, and getting faster about it. His panic is the quickest thing in the whole pack.

cards.title: Ruby
cards.omen: Made of love, worth a fortune.
cards.lore: Pressed from a thousand tiny hearts into the deck's most literal treasure. Adore him and he multiplies; ignore him and he goes dark and mean.

cards.title: Bouquet
cards.omen: Hands up. Here's a bouquet.
cards.lore: He robs the season blind and pays it back in petals, wanted in all four quarters and forgiven in each. Hands up — here's a flower.

cards.title: Seer
cards.omen: I've read the ending. It's fine.
cards.lore: He opened the third eye somewhere past the last bridge and now he won't stop narrating the ending. Each gold ring is worth exactly one true, terrible prophecy.

cards.title: Portal
cards.omen: Every door's a wound in the deck.
cards.lore: He cuts holes between packs and steps through with the good cards and a bad attitude. The coin at his throat opens whatever it touches, whether you wanted it open or not.

cards.title: Lunar
cards.omen: The moon says draw. So draw.
cards.lore: Half fowl, half shadow, answering only to the moon and never to reason. A full moon doubles his floor and triples his temper.

cards.title: Fuzz
cards.omen: We're definitely not connected.
cards.lore: Green holds orange holds blue, and all three swear on their lives they aren't touching. Wager the top and the whole tower wobbles into the Fear.

cards.title: Pilgrim
cards.omen: Walking since the first edition.
cards.lore: He walked in from a set that stopped printing, staff worn smooth by the highway. Whatever pack he joins gains a little dust and a little grace.

cards.title: Shrug
cards.omen: Eh. It's all just cards.
cards.lore: Offered the secrets of the universe, he shrugs and asks what's for lunch. His indifference is, somehow, load-bearing.

cards.title: Fractal
cards.omen: A smile within a smile within—
cards.lore: His grin repeats inside itself forever, smaller and smaller and never finished. Stare too long and you're a card wedged in his teeth.

cards.title: Alchemist
cards.omen: Money's just slow fire.
cards.lore: He turns burns into fortunes and fortunes into smoke without breaking eye contact. The bill in his hands is worth exactly what the chain swears today and nothing tomorrow.

cards.title: Reacher
cards.omen: One more. Just one more.
cards.lore: Always grabbing for the card just outside the frame with hands too big to trust. Whatever he can touch, he counts as his.

cards.title: Overqualified
cards.omen: Statistically, you're in trouble.
cards.lore: Overdressed, overqualified, and calculating odds nobody asked for. In a fight he just explains, precisely, why you've already lost.

cards.title: Sourpuss
cards.omen: Fine. Whatever. Draw.
cards.lore: Perpetually unimpressed and dragging his mood into every pack he touches. Win him and he sulks; lose him and he sulks harder.

cards.title: Ascendant
cards.omen: Up and out. Don't wait up.
cards.lore: Too cool to explain where he's floating off to above the wreckage. Own him and the whole season's about to lift off.

cards.title: Bog
cards.omen: The bottom is a place. I live there.
cards.lore: Dragged up from the swamp gas at the bottom of the market and chained in a gold frame so he can't crawl back down. One eye stays open, always, watching the floor.

cards.title: Sonder
cards.omen: Everyone here is the main character.
cards.lore: He looked out at the whole degenerate crowd and understood, all at once, that every last one had a weekend as ruined as his. Then he put the shades back on and ordered another.

cards.title: Grapefruit
cards.omen: Citrus and speed — the only clean meal.
cards.lore: Breakfast was two grapefruits, a fistful of uppers, and the sports page. He has not come down since and has no travel plans.

cards.title: Rug
cards.omen: They always pull it. Stand anyway.
cards.lore: He stands on the finest rug in the room precisely because he knows they'll pull it. Might as well admire the pattern on the way to the floor.

cards.title: Linen
cards.omen: Clean robe, filthy ledger.
cards.lore: Dressed all in white like a man with nothing left to confess, which is its own kind of lie. The dog knows. The dog always knows.

cards.title: Neon
cards.omen: The vacancy sign never lies.
cards.lore: Lit from outside by a sign that says nothing and means less, he smokes and waits for the frame to buzz out. Motel light, 4 AM, forever.

cards.title: Sepia
cards.omen: Nostalgia is just old fear.
cards.lore: Photographed brown and cracked like he's already a memory somebody's trying to fence. The dog came free with the frame.

cards.title: Tango
cards.omen: It takes two to lose this badly.
cards.lore: Two partners, one bad idea, spinning across the felt while the music runs out. Neither will admit who's leading this catastrophe.

cards.title: Panther
cards.omen: Steal the cool. Return nothing.
cards.lore: He runs with borrowed cartoons because his own reflection got repossessed. Cool, pink, and completely unaccounted for.

cards.title: Tabby
cards.omen: Half-drawn and twice as proud.
cards.lore: A stray out of a set that never finished printing, holding a pose nobody asked for. She sits in the frame like she paid for it.

cards.title: Tux
cards.omen: Black tie. Blacker odds.
cards.lore: Dressed for a funeral that might be his own, holding number twenty-two like a losing raffle ticket. The house always dresses well.

cards.title: Tropic
cards.omen: Dress tropical, think criminal.
cards.lore: He wore the loudest shirt in the hemisphere to a very quiet crime. The flowers are the decoy; the cigarette is the tell.

cards.title: Geezer
cards.omen: I got old waiting for the turn.
cards.lore: Aged forty years in one long weekend and wears every hour of it on his face. He's seen this number come up before and it never once paid.

cards.title: Vermillion
cards.omen: Everything's redder after midnight.
cards.lore: Bathed in a red that isn't paint and isn't blood but splits the difference. He smokes like the color owes him money.

cards.title: Vapor
cards.omen: The sun sets in low resolution.
cards.lore: Retired to a beach that only exists at 8-bit sunset, pension paid in dead pixels. He earned this. He thinks.

cards.title: Beard
cards.omen: Everything's hidden but the hands.
cards.lore: He grew the beard to hide the tell and the tell just moved to his hands. Now he smokes and dares you to read him.

cards.title: Robe
cards.omen: Checkout was hours ago. Keep walking.
cards.lore: Walked out of the casino in a bathrobe at noon and kept right on walking into the desert. The dog followed. Bat country does that.

cards.title: Duke
cards.omen: We can't stop here. This is bat country.
cards.lore: The full costume — bucket hat, savage goggles, a cigarette holder clenched like a weapon. He is the Fear, and he brought his own.

cards.title: Checker
cards.omen: Your move. It was always your move.
cards.lore: He conducts his business on a checkerboard floor because everything he does is one move from lost. Black square, white square, no square safe.

cards.title: Ember
cards.omen: Naughty, nice — all combustible.
cards.lore: Red hood, white beard, and a list of who's been holding and who's been dumping. He brings the burn whether you were good or not.

cards.title: Paisley
cards.omen: Stare at the paisley. It stares back.
cards.lore: Upholstered in a pattern that moves when you aren't looking, which is most of the time. The cats are just accessories to the swirl.

cards.title: Collage
cards.omen: I'm a collage of unpaid tabs.
cards.lore: A whole ransacked scrapbook of a man, taped back together from the good years and the felonies. The cat reads the evidence aloud.

cards.title: Sketch
cards.omen: Still just an outline of trouble.
cards.lore: Caught as pure line before the color and the crimes set in, innocent as a first draft. Hold him and you inherit whatever he becomes.

cards.title: Whiskers
cards.omen: Draw the cat. Pay the frog.
cards.lore: Three strays on one blue tin ticket, none of them related and all of them wanted. Loteria for degenerates.

cards.title: Haze
cards.omen: You can't see the odds through this.
cards.lore: The smoke got so thick the whole card went purple and the little ones stopped asking questions. Breathe shallow.

cards.title: Ten
cards.omen: Ten of something. Nobody's sure what.
cards.lore: Numbered ten in a set that never agreed on nine, pastel and proud of it. Soft colors, hard habit.

cards.title: Fedora
cards.omen: No name. Just the hat.
cards.lore: Hat pulled low, shades on at night, a face the deck can't place and won't insure. He came in from an older, meaner set.

cards.title: Redeye
cards.omen: Red light means go, apparently.
cards.lore: Three days awake and the eyes went to stoplights. In a silver suit he still looks like the last thing left at the party.

cards.title: Chain
cards.omen: Heavy hangs the chain that flexes.
cards.lore: He wears the whole floor's gold around his neck and calls it insurance. The goggles keep the glare of his own success out of his eyes.

cards.title: Silver
cards.omen: I was rich before you were printed.
cards.lore: Silvered at the temples and unbothered by any of it, he's outlasted four seasons and every man who bet against them. Old money, older habits.

cards.title: Glitch
cards.omen: I'm a mistake they can't reprint.
cards.lore: Something went wrong in the press and he came out flickering — half here, half last frame. The error is load-bearing now.

cards.title: Free
cards.omen: Nothing costs more than free.
cards.lore: Marked FREE because nobody would pay and everybody wanted him anyway. The most expensive word in the whole deck.

cards.title: Crowd
cards.omen: We're all him. Good luck.
cards.lore: Somewhere in this mob of identical degenerates is the real one, and he isn't telling. Wager him and you wager all of them.

cards.title: Velvet
cards.omen: Draw the curtain. Same act.
cards.lore: Posed against velvet like a lounge act that never got the light, bow tie crooked with contempt. He'll perform for a burn.

cards.title: Reefer
cards.omen: Relax. It's medicinal. Mostly.
cards.lore: Dressed head to toe in the merchandise, which is either confidence or a confession. Slow to the duel and slower to care.

cards.title: Bowtie
cards.omen: Overdressed. Underconcerned.
cards.lore: Dressed up for a game he doesn't understand and winning it anyway, bow tie and all. The pink isn't a choice, it's a fate.

cards.title: Spawn
cards.omen: One card, three problems.
cards.lore: Three of them where there should be one, blinking up from the purple with twenty little hearts between them. The deck multiplies when you aren't watching.

cards.title: Cloud
cards.omen: Up here the floor can't reach me.
cards.lore: Ascended on a pink cloud with a walking cane and no forwarding address, too pleased with himself to explain. Own him and the whole weekend floats off.
